clusters.title: Treasury officially responds: Claims hackers behind theft of funds

versions.title: Treasury officially responds: Claims hackers behind theft of funds
versions.deck: The Finance, Planning and Economic Development Ministry yesterday said cyber hackers had breached its systems, resulting in a reported loss of $ 2.5 million which was part of a bilateral payment to Australia.

clusters.title: Treasury rocked by $ 2.5 m fraud

versions.title: Treasury rocked by $ 2.5 m fraud
versions.deck: The Government is facing scrutiny following reports that $ 2.5 million (nearly Rs. 800 million) of Treasury funds was diverted during a foreign debt repayment, with the amount yet to reach the intended creditor country.

clusters.title: EPF assets rise 13.6% to Rs. 5 t in 2025

versions.title: EPF assets rise 13.6% to Rs. 5 t in 2025
versions.deck: The Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) recorded a 13.6% increase in net worth to Rs. 4.9 trillion at end-2025, up from Rs. 4.4 trillion a year earlier, according to the Central Bank of Sri Lanka’s (CBSL) Annual Economic Review 2025.

clusters.title: EPF equity portfolio value up 27% to Rs. 188.9 b in 2025

versions.title: EPF equity portfolio value up 27% to Rs. 188.9 b in 2025
versions.deck: The Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) recorded a sharp increase in the market value of its listed equity portfolio in 2025, rising to Rs. 188.85 billion as at 31 December from Rs. 148.25 billion a year earlier, reflecting a broad-based recovery in share prices.

clusters.title: Rs. 5 b Janashakthi IPO draws over Rs. 15 b in bids; heavy pro-rata cuts as demand tops 3x

versions.title: Rs. 5 b Janashakthi IPO draws over Rs. 15 b in bids; heavy pro-rata cuts as demand tops 3x
versions.deck: Janashakthi Ltd., (JXG) yesterday announced the basis of allotment for its Rs. 5 billion Initial Public Offering (IPO), which was oversubscribed by more than three times on its opening day on 9 April.

clusters.title: Mideast war takes toll on tea, March exports down 16% YoY

versions.title: Mideast war takes toll on tea, March exports down 16% YoY
versions.deck: Asia Siyaka Commodities yesterday said the escalation of tensions between the US, Israel, and Iran significantly impacted tea exports in March, following the bombing of Iran on 28 February and subsequent retaliatory attacks on Israel and the Gulf States.

clusters.title: Cabinet approves Rs. 356 m Kelani Valley Railway Line development project deal

versions.title: Cabinet approves Rs. 356 m Kelani Valley Railway Line development project deal
versions.deck: The Cabinet of Ministers this week approved the awarding of the extension of the Kelani Valley Railway Line from Avissawella to Ratnapura contract worth Rs. 356.22 million (exclusive of taxes) to Resources Development Consultants Ltd.

clusters.title: Mideast war drives food, fuel and forex risks despite recovery: WFP

versions.title: Mideast war drives food, fuel and forex risks despite recovery: WFP
versions.deck: A latest assessment warns that volatility in the Gulf is transmitting across multiple channels, including energy, food, agriculture, and external earnings, with direct implications for inflation, balance of payments stability, and household consumption.

clusters.title: Inadequate LPG price hike compels poor to subsidise wealthy: Advocata

versions.title: Inadequate LPG price hike compels poor to subsidise wealthy: Advocata
versions.deck: Advocata Institute yesterday warned that the recent increase in domestic Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) prices remains insufficient, effectively shifting the cost burden onto lower-income households through indirect cross-subsidies.

clusters.title: Per capita GDP hits all-time high of $ 5,003 in 2025

versions.title: Per capita GDP hits all-time high of $ 5,003 in 2025
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s per capita GDP at current market prices rose to an all-time high of $ 5,003 in 2025, extending the recovery from the contraction seen during the economic crisis.

clusters.title: CSE foreign outflow tops Rs. 25 b YTD

clusters.title: Jaswar outlines his plans

versions.title: Jaswar outlines his plans
versions.deck: Former Royalist Jaswar Umar has outlined a clear and progressive vision for the future of the National Olympic Committee, focusing on transparency, athlete welfare, and stronger collaboration with National Federations. He is one of the top candidates to take over the Presidency.

versions.title: Athapaththu returns from injury to lead Sri Lanka to series-levelling win
versions.deck: Sri Lanka Women’s cricket Captain Chamari Athapaththu returned from a finger injury to lead her team to a series-levelling four-wicket win against Bangladesh in the second WODI played at Rajshahi yesterday.

clusters.title: Why systems thinking is the next step for clinical governance in Sri Lanka

versions.title: Why systems thinking is the next step for clinical governance in Sri Lanka
versions.deck: A child presents to a busy private clinic with persistent headaches. The consultation is brief. There is no structured documentation template, no escalation pathway and no system to ensure follow-up. Weeks later, the family seeks help elsewhere with worsening symptoms.

clusters.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka

versions.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka
versions.deck: The Rangiri Dambulla Cricket Stadium will be the venue for New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team’s six-match tour of Sri Lanka next month. clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis.
